member.name: Alison Griffiths
member.role: MP for Bognor Regis and Littlehampton
member.synopsis: Alison Griffiths is the Conservative MP for Bognor Regis and Littlehampton , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Water Companies: Ombudsman
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what the timetable is for establishing a statutory Water Ombudsman; what assessment she has made of the powers required to provide effective redress to customers; and whether she has m
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government will establish the Water Ombudsman at the earliest opportunity. The Government is applying best practice from other sectors, such as energy, communications, finance and rail, to develop a model which includes the powers required to provide ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Water Companies: Regulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what timetable she has set for ending operator self-monitoring by water companies; what assessment she has made of the additional data, staffing, laboratory, digital and enforcement ca
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Environment Agency is making a step change in regulatory scrutiny across the sector, moving away from operator self-monitoring towards a more transparent, intelligence-led approach to monitoring. This includes increased inspections, independent audits...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Audiology: Health Services

writtenQuestions.heading: Audiology: Standards

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department of Health and Social Care has indicated that it will not be possible to answer this question within the usual time period. An answer is being prepared and will be provided as soon as it is available.

writtenQuestions.answerText: At the Budget, the VO announced updated property values from the 2026 revaluation. This revaluation is the first since the pandemic, which has led to significant increases in rateable values for some properties. In recognition of the impact of the revalu...

writtenQuestions.heading: Maternity Services: Sussex
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether his Department has made an assessment of disparities in maternity outcomes across Sussex by (a) ethnicity, (b) deprivation level and (c) geography.
writtenQuestions.answerText: From January to December 2025, the rate of postpartum haemorrhage of 1.5 litres or more at the University Hospitals Sussex NHS Foundation Trust (UHSx) was highest for Black or Black British women, at 45.6 per 1,000 deliveries, followed by Asian or Asian B...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Maternity Services: University Hospitals Sussex NHS Foundation Trust
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of trends in the level of serious maternity incidents at University Hospitals Sussex NHS Foundation Trust since 2018.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Stabilised and adjusted perinatal mortality rates at University Sussex Hospitals in 2024 remained unchanged from 2020, at 5.49 per 1,000 births. In both time periods, the trust perinatal mortality rates were 5% to 15% lower than comparable trusts. However...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Maternity Services: University Hospitals Sussex NHS Foundation Trust
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what mechanisms are in place to ensure that lessons identified by the Donna Ockenden review into maternity services in Sussex are implemented by NHS organisations.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Sussex families were closely involved in the design of the review through meetings with the former Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, in March and April 2026. Donna Ockenden has since been appointed as Chair of the Independent review into mate...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Maternity Services: University Hospitals Sussex NHS Foundation Trust
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to ensure that families affected by maternity care failings in Sussex can engage effectively with the independent review chaired by Donna Ockenden.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Sussex families were closely involved in the design of the review through meetings with the former Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, in March and April 2026. Donna Ockenden has since been appointed as Chair of the Independent review into mate...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Maternity Services: University Hospitals Sussex NHS Foundation Trust
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether his Department has made an assessment of maternity staffing levels at University Hospitals Sussex NHS Foundation Trust since 2018.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As of March 2026, there are 409 full time equivalent midwives working in the University Hospitals Sussex NHS Foundation Trust. This is 20, or 5.2%, more than last year and 20, or 5.2%, more than in March 2021.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
